That's the full picture. Here's the structured report.

### SUMMARY
Explored `java/wotb-core/src/main/java/com/wotb/core/` — the module contains replay parsing, battle result data models, Excel export, rating/stats computation, and reference data (map names, tankopedia). The module has **no custom base classes, no enums, and no custom exception types**. It uses plain POJOs with public fields (`Battle`, `PlayerResult`, `Agg`), Java records (`Source`, `TankInfo`, `Columns.Column`), and a single top-level constants file (`Columns.java`). `wotb-web` depends on these model classes directly; there is no abstract `BaseEntity` or shared interface hierarchy.

### EVIDENCE
- `java/wotb-core/src/main/java/com/wotb/core/Columns.java` (root level): a `public final class Columns` with a `Column` record and static lists (`IDENTITY`, `STAT`, `TAIL`, `PLAYER`) that define Excel column headers and data keys. This is the only top-level constants/shared surface file.
- `java/wotb-core/src/main/java/com/wotb/core/model/` contains 6 files: `Agg.java` (aggregated player stats), `Battle.java` (single battle with metadata + `List<PlayerResult>`), `Collected.java` (multi-battle dedup container), `PlayerResult.java` (full per-player per-battle result with public fields), `Source.java` (record: name + bytes), `TankInfo.java` (record: tank metadata).
- `java/wotb-core/src/main/java/com/wotb/core/parse/` contains 5 files: `EventStreamReader.java`, `PickleReader.java`, `Protobuf.java`, `ReplayParser.java`, `Replays.java` — the replay parsing pipeline.
- `java/wotb-core/src/main/java/com/wotb/core/ref/` contains `MapNames.java` (arena ID→name) and `Tankopedia.java` (tank ID→TankInfo) — static reference/lookup utilities.
- `java/wotb-core/src/main/java/com/wotb/core/stats/` contains `Aggregator.java`, `Players.java`, `PotentialDamage.java`, `Rating.java`, `RatingAnalyzer.java`, `RatingConfig.java` — rating computation and cross-battle aggregation.
- `java/wotb-core/src/main/java/com/wotb/core/export/` contains `ExcelExporter.java`, `ExcelStyles.java`, `SingleBattleSheets.java`, `AggregateSheets.java` — Excel output (Apache POI).
- **No enums** (`enum` keyword absent from all 22 `.java` files in the module).
- **No base/abstract classes** — model classes use inheritance only from `java.lang.Object`, no `abstract` or `extends`.
- **No custom exception types** — only standard Java exceptions (`IOException`, `IllegalArgumentException`, `IndexOutOfBoundsException`, etc.).
